batch2_incumbent.label: actual incumbent after resume batch2 / relay_l9y0p75_l12y0p25_on_entry

hypotheses_tested.summary: Hold the incumbent l9 = 0.75 s soft gate fixed and probe only smaller / larger / asymmetric l12 terminal reclosures. This directly tests whether the new winner is sitting on a narrow terminal timing ridge or just at one coarse-grid lucky point.

Retune l9 slightly below/above 0.75 s, first with the incumbent l12 = 0.25 s micro-closure and then with the directionally expected companion l12 adjustment (lower l9 -> slightly heavier closure, higher l9 -> slightly lighter closure).

The asymmetrically split l12 probes are the physically meaningful micro-hybrid here: they keep the prompt second point backbone (`l8 x1 + l9 0.75 + unified core`) and only add a tiny directionally biased terminal reclosure, instead of reopening broader side families.

rows_sorted.rationale: Coupled higher-l9 / lighter-l12 variant: if later l9 already supplies more y-information, the terminal closure may need to back off proportionally.
Raise the l9 gate slightly above 0.75 s while keeping the incumbent l12 = 0.25 s, to test the upper side of the same compatibility ridge.
Lower the l9 gate slightly below 0.75 s while keeping the incumbent l12 = 0.25 s, to test whether the coarse-grid optimum is drifting mildly downward.
Coupled lower-l9 / heavier-l12 variant: if earlier l9 closure slightly under-excites the terminal correction, a somewhat stronger l12 reclosure may compensate.
Same incumbent backbone, but strengthen the terminal l12 dwell from 0.25 s to 0.375 s on each leg to test the opposite side of the local terminal ridge.

Keep the total terminal dwell equal to the incumbent (0.5 s total), but front-load it on the outgoing l12 leg (0.375 / 0.125). This checks whether the useful terminal information is concentrated before exact closure rather than after it.
Reverse the same total terminal dwell split (0.125 / 0.375) to test whether any asymmetry benefit is directional or just due to total time.
Same incumbent backbone, but halve the terminal l12 dwell from 0.25 s to 0.125 s on each leg to test whether the current winner is still slightly over-closed at the terminal y ridge.

best_candidate.rationale: Coupled higher-l9 / lighter-l12 variant: if later l9 already supplies more y-information, the terminal closure may need to back off proportionally.

near_misses.rationale: Lower the l9 gate slightly below 0.75 s while keeping the incumbent l12 = 0.25 s, to test whether the coarse-grid optimum is drifting mildly downward.
Coupled lower-l9 / heavier-l12 variant: if earlier l9 closure slightly under-excites the terminal correction, a somewhat stronger l12 reclosure may compensate.
Same incumbent backbone, but strengthen the terminal l12 dwell from 0.25 s to 0.375 s on each leg to test the opposite side of the local terminal ridge.

Keep the total terminal dwell equal to the incumbent (0.5 s total), but front-load it on the outgoing l12 leg (0.375 / 0.125). This checks whether the useful terminal information is concentrated before exact closure rather than after it.

kf36_gate_reason: No candidate was close enough to the incumbent to justify KF36.

bottom_line.statement: A fresh local improvement was found even after the batch2 win; the corrected frontier still had headroom inside the narrow l9/l12 ridge.
